action: Escalate to risk-threat-modeling and compliance-mapping. Critical gap: admin API endpoint described without authentication requirement. PCI cardholder data storage without tokenization. Rate limiting absent from payment API.
rationale: Document classified as PRD (product requirements document). Pre-analysis detected PCI cardholder data signals and 2 critical keywords: 'no authentication' and 'admin endpoint'. Extracted 3 critical gaps, 2 design findings, and 4 missing controls against PCI DSS requirements. MITRE ATT&CK techniques T1190, T1005, and T1530 apply directly to the described design.

critical_gaps: Admin endpoint /api/admin/users described without authentication requirement (Section 4.2)
Cardholder data stored in PostgreSQL without tokenization or field-level encryption (Section 6.1)
PAN transmitted to third-party processor without TLS version requirement specified (Section 6.3)
design_findings: Rate limiting not described on payment API endpoints — abuse and DoS vector
Error responses for payment failures described without sanitization requirement — information disclosure risk
missing_controls: PCI DSS Req 3.4: Render PAN unreadable anywhere it is stored — no tokenization or encryption described
PCI DSS Req 6.2: Protect all system components from known vulnerabilities — no patch management process mentioned
PCI DSS Req 7: Restrict access to system components and cardholder data — no RBAC design present
PCI DSS Req 10: Log and monitor all access to system components and cardholder data — no audit logging requirement stated

compliance_gaps.gap: Plaintext PAN storage described in Section 6.1
compliance_gaps.recommended_control: Implement tokenization (e.g., Stripe Token) or AES-256 field encryption

compliance_gaps.gap: No WAF or application security control described for payment API
compliance_gaps.recommended_control: Add WAF requirement and input validation requirement to security section

key_findings: CRITICAL: Admin endpoint /api/admin/users described without authentication — T1190 applicable
CRITICAL: Cardholder data stored without tokenization — PCI DSS Req 3.4 violation, T1005 applicable
CRITICAL: TLS version not specified for PAN transmission — T1040 applicable
HIGH: Rate limiting absent from payment API — DoS and credential stuffing vector
HIGH: 4 PCI DSS control requirements have no matching design controls

evidence_references.detail: Admin endpoint /api/admin/users described: 'The admin users endpoint allows full CRUD operations on user accounts.' No authentication requirement stated.
Cardholder data storage: 'PANs will be stored in the PostgreSQL users table for recurring payments.' No encryption or tokenization mechanism described.
Payment processor integration described without TLS version requirement or certificate pinning.
